more.title: LNG Canada offers five First Nations option to invest up to C$1bn in Phase 2 storage tank
more.description: LNG Canada said on Tuesday five First Nations will have an equity option to invest up to C$1-billion ($710.78-million) to acquire a storage tank planned to be built in a proposed expansion of the country's first major liquefied natural gas (LNG) export facility. First Nations, Canada's indigenous peoples groups, have been buying stakes in the country's energy projects seeking economic benefits from projects built on their land, amid rising capital requirements for companies in the sector.

more.title: Cele says he wasn’t aware of outstanding TRC cases when he headed SAPS

more.title: SAPS assembles team to probe murder of March and March Gauteng leader
more.description: The South African Police Service has appointed a multidisciplinary team of detectives and crime intelligence officers to investigate the murder of March and March Gauteng leader, Mr Andile Mvuyiselwa Somgaxa.Murder detailsAccording to SAPS, Mr Somgaxa was shot in the driveway of his home in Greenfield, Johannesburg, on *03 July 2026*. He succumbed to his injuries in hospital on July 13 2026.The team was assembled by Acting National Commissioner Lieutenant General Puleng Dimpane in consultation with Gauteng Provincial Commissioner Lieutenant General Tommy Mthombeni.Lieutenant General Dimpane...

more.title: AI won’t save you in a SARS audit
more.description: South African companies are buying AI-powered compliance tools faster than they're reading the regulation the tools are supposed to satisfy, writes BRADLEY ELLIOTT, CEO of RelyComply. The post AI won’t save you in a SARS audit appeared first on Gadget .

more.title: South Africa’s August fuel price cuts shrink as Middle East tensions push oil higher
more.description: South African motorists hoping for another sizeable drop in fuel prices next month may need to temper their expectations, with the renewed conflict in the Middle East likely to increase global oil prices, Cape {town} Etc reports. According to MyBroadband, South Africa adjusts fuel prices every month using a formula based largely on international oil prices and the rand’s exchange rate... Read more → The post South Africa’s August fuel price cuts shrink as Middle East tensions push oil higher appeared first on Cape Town ETC .

more.title: NUM expresses strong disapproval of jobs cuts by De Beers in South Africa
more.description: Labour union the National Union of Mineworkers (NUM) has strongly decried the plans announced by multinational diamond company De Beers and De Beers Sightholder Sales South Africa (DBSSSA) to pause production at the Venetia mine, in Limpopo, for two years and to issue a Section 189A notice to employees. The NUM says this decision threatens the livelihoods of 1 134 permanent employees at the Ventia mine and a further 80 employees at DBSSSA.
